questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: Sam: The weather report says it’s very likely to rain tomorrow. Jim: Oh, no! They’ll cancel the game if it rains. Sam: Jim: Yeah. Let’s do so.
options: Let’s just keep our fingers crossed hoping it won’t rain tomorrow.
You should let it go and let bygones be bygones.
Let me take a rain check because I can’t make it tomorrow.
Suit yourself since it never rains but it pours.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: Dave: There’re many reasons for me not to go to college, Dad. Dad: Do tell.
options: I have no time for your crap.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: Jackson: This hot, humid weather is killing me! Susan: It’s so different from my hometown. Jackson: Where are you from? Susan: London.
options: Why don’t you turn on the heat?

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: (On the phone) Clerk: This is room service. May I help you? Mr. Brown: Clerk: Sure, sir. Your food will be ready in about 15 minutes. Mr. Brown: Please bring them as soon as possible. I’m very hungry.
options: I’d like to have someone to clean my room. I spilt my milk.
Do you have any single room available?
I’m sorry. I’ve dialed the wrong number.
I’d like to order a sandwich and a cup of juice.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: (At the customs) Customs officer: Do you have anything to declare? Joyce: Yes, I have these five bottles of wine. They’re gifts from friends. Customs: Joyce: About $150 in total.
options: How much did you pay for them?
How much duty did you charge?
Do you have any food or vegetable?

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: (At the train station) Passenger A: Excuse me. Could you show me how to use this machine? Passenger B: Sure. Where are you going? Passenger A: Taipei. Passenger B: Passenger A: Thanks a lot.
options: Just press this button and put in fifty dollars.
Just go down the street and walk for three blocks.
I think you’re late, and you’d better hurry up.
All you need is take Bus 305 and get on at Taipei station.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: Chief Manager: Are you for selling this product in America? David: Chief Manager: Do you think the marketing strategy is all right? David: It’s absolutely all right.
options: This product is not produced in Europe.
I’m sure it’s wise to do so.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: (At a police station) Pete: I have to report a theft. Officer: What was taken? Pete: I had my wallet stolen with my passport. Officer: How much money were you carrying? Pete: About $500 in cash. Officer: I wouldn’t count on it.
options: Is there any possibility of getting my passport back?
I was going to a movie with my best friend.
I’d like to consult a Chinese doctor if possible.
He’s unconscious. Could you give him first aid?

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: Hess: I stayed up so late last night and I’m not feeling well now. What do you reckon I should do? Helen: Hess: I know. But what should I do now? Helen: Perhaps you should take a day off and rest. Hess: I think you’re right. I’ll never make such a mistake again.
options: That sounds like a great idea.
The grass is greener on the other side of the fence.
You shouldn’t have burned the midnight oil.
I wish I had the answer, too.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: Michael: I’m thinking about finding a second job to earn some extra money. Judith: Why? Michael: Yeah, but I’m still not paid well. I suppose I can find a part-time job as a waiter at night. Judith: Having two jobs is excruciating. You might feel exhausted. Michael: I know. But my parents can’t afford to send me to study abroad. I have to earn and save as much as money as possible.
options: Didn’t you just quit your job?
I thought you just got a raise. III. 綜合測驗 ( 第 21-30 題,每題 2 分,共 20 分 ) 說明:以下兩篇短文,共有 10 個空格,為第 21 至 30 題,請依各篇短文文意,選出一個最適合 該空格的答案 。

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: Why does Mrs. Fozdar say “Mankind is like a bird and the bird has two wings. One is man, and the other is woman”?
options: To illustrate her belief that men and women should be equal.
To describe her dream for mankind to fly like a bird.
To encourage mankind to set themselves free.
To explain her biological study comparing men with birds.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: What is the main idea of this passage?
options: Mankind is like birds because they both have two sexes.
Old people should stay in their rocking chairs and relax.
A man should love his wives equally, no matter how many he has.
Mrs. Fozdar’s fighting for women’s rights and her efforts against polygamy.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: What is true about polygamy?
options: It’s the practice of having more than one spouse at one time.
It’s practiced around the world except among Muslims.
It’s a practice supported by Mrs. Fozdar after she moved to Singapore.
The Koran limited the number of wives a man might have to five.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: Which of the following is TRUE?
options: Mrs. Fozdar began to fight against polygamy after she got divorced.
It’s well-known that Muslims have always ignored women’s rights.
Mrs. Fozdar has started to fight for women’s right until she was 83.
Mrs. Fozdar’s fighting for women’s right has lasted for more than 50 years.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: Which of the following is the best to describe Mrs. Fozdar?
options: Indifferent. ▲ 閱讀下文後,作答第 36-40 題 The explosion of hip-hop onto the music scene in the 1990s was the biggest musical revolution in American pop history. That’s according to a team of scientists who published their findings in the Royal Society Open Journal.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: What’s the main idea of this passage?
options: The rise of new technologies has initiated many new types of music.
The emergence of hip-hop was the biggest revolution in American pop music.
The three most important revolutions in American history.
Why bands like The Beatles and Rolling Stones were popular.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: The phrase ushered in is similar in meaning to the phrase.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: How many distinct music revolutions are mentioned in this passage?

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: According to the passage, which of the following statements is true?
options: The Beatles and Rolling stones were important bands that initiated new rock trends.
Music has always played an important part in American revolutions.
Rap is one of the most important genres in modern American pop music.
Duran Duran was one of the most important bands during the first American pop revolution.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: What is true about the band “the Beatles”?
options: They were from the United States and soon got extremely popular.
They were extremely popular in the United States during the 1960s.
They used new technologies such as synthesizers, samplers and drum machine.

They belonged to the same music generation as Eurythmics. 第二部分:非選擇題 ( 第 1 至 5 題,每題 4 分,共 20 分 ) I. 填充 說明:▲請依據中文提示,在空格內填入適當英文字以完成句子。 ▲每格限填一字,超過一字者視為錯誤,不予計分 。 ▲請將答案(空格中單字)依序寫在答案卷之 「 非選擇題作答區」 之指定範圍內,請勿抄題。
